title: atractocarpus pterocarpon
intro: Atractocarpus pterocarpon is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the 'tree of life' due to its widespread medicinal uses. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Atractocarpus pterocarpon include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health, making it a sought-after herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Atractocarpus pterocarpon can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the leaves and bark being the most commonly used parts for herbal preparations.
